domains.label: Demographics and Physical Characteristics
domains.definition: Basic biological, physical, and socio-economic attributes of the participants.

subdomains.label: Personal Attributes
subdomains.definition: Core demographic and physical identifiers of the individual.

features.label: Age
features.definition: Age of participant in years.

features.label: Biological sex
features.definition: Self-reported biological sex (male/female).

features.label: Handedness
features.definition: Left- or right-handed.

features.label: Body mass index
features.definition: Body mass index (kg/m^2). Zero values are data errors and treated as missing.

subdomains.label: Socioeconomic Status
subdomains.definition: Indicators of educational attainment and family background.

features.label: Education level
features.definition: Highest completed education level (CBS classification): low/medium/high.

features.label: Background socio-economic status
features.definition: Background SES from parental income and education (range 2-6).

domains.label: Psychometric Profiles
domains.definition: Standardized questionnaire scores measuring personality, motivation, and anxiety.

subdomains.label: Big Five Personality
subdomains.definition: Scores from the NEO-FFI inventory assessing the five-factor model of personality.

features.label: Neuroticism (NEO-FFI)
features.definition: NEO-FFI Neuroticism scale (sum of items).

features.label: Extraversion (NEO-FFI)
features.definition: NEO-FFI Extraversion scale (sum of items).

features.label: Openness (NEO-FFI)
features.definition: NEO-FFI Openness to experience scale (sum of items).

features.label: Agreeableness (NEO-FFI)
features.definition: NEO-FFI Agreeableness scale (sum of items).

features.label: Conscientiousness (NEO-FFI)
features.definition: NEO-FFI Conscientiousness scale (sum of items).

subdomains.label: Reinforcement Sensitivity
subdomains.definition: Scores from the BIS/BAS scales measuring behavioral inhibition and activation systems.

features.label: BAS drive
features.definition: Behavioural Activation System drive subscale.

features.label: BAS fun-seeking
features.definition: Behavioural Activation System fun-seeking subscale.

features.label: BAS reward responsiveness
features.definition: Behavioural Activation System reward responsiveness subscale.

features.label: Behavioural inhibition (BIS)
features.definition: Behavioural Inhibition System scale.

subdomains.label: Anxiety Traits
subdomains.definition: Scores from the STAI-T inventory measuring stable trait anxiety.

features.label: Trait anxiety (STAI-T)
features.definition: State-Trait Anxiety Inventory, trait subscale.

domains.label: Identity and Belief Systems
domains.definition: Self-reported ratings regarding sexual orientation, gender identity, and religious affiliation.

subdomains.label: Sexual and Gender Identity
subdomains.definition: Subjective ratings of sexual attraction and gender identification.

features.label: Sexual attraction to males
features.definition: Degree of sexual attraction to males (1-7).

features.label: Sexual attraction to females
features.definition: Degree of sexual attraction to females (1-7).

features.label: Gender identity (male)
features.definition: Degree to which the subject felt male (1-7).

features.label: Gender identity (female)
features.definition: Degree to which the subject felt female (1-7).

subdomains.label: Religiosity
subdomains.definition: Measures of religious upbringing, current practice, and personal importance of faith.

features.label: Religious upbringing
features.definition: Whether the subject had a religious upbringing (yes/no).

features.label: Currently religious
features.definition: Whether the subject is religious now (yes/no).

features.label: Importance of religion
features.definition: How much religion plays a role in the subject's life (1-5).

context: AOMIC ID1000: 928 healthy Dutch young adults. Features are self-report questionnaire scores (NEO-FFI Big Five personality, BIS/BAS reinforcement sensitivity, STAI trait anxiety), demographics, anthropometrics, socio-economic status, sexual/gender identity ratings, and religiosity. Group scales from the same instrument together.
